---
title: "Map Gallery"
format:
  html:
    theme: superhero
    css: styles.css
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-title: "On this page"
    toc-depth: 3
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

<h2>[Proposal of examining The Species Composition of Petawawa Research
Forest]{.text-primary}</h2>

::::: columns
::: {.column width="30%"}
<h5>Research & Area of Interest:</h5>

This project utilizes airborne laser scanning (ALS) data and provided
climate databases, combined with Python-based Classification tools
(sklearn-RandomForestClassifier), to rank dominant tree species within
the Petawawa Research Forest (PRF) while investigating their approximate
distribution patterns.
:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="65%"}
![](images/petawawa_study_area.jpg){width="100%"} Research Area Overview
:::
:::::

::::: columns
::: {.column width="65%"}
![](images/petawawa.png){width="100%"}
<br>
![](images/rank.png){width="100%"} Both the map and histogram were
generated using Python.
:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
<h5>Result:</h5>

The results are quite clear: white pine is the most dominant species.
However, it is worth noting that the current model is constrained by the
limited training ground plots, resulting in an overall accuracy of only
58%. Furthermore, the model incorrectly classifies unvegetated areas,
such as water bodies, as a single species distribution. This aspect
requires improvement. Nevertheless, the expected results still
demonstrate the feasibility of our approach.
:::
:::::


<br>
<br>


<h2>[The Most Livable Space in the Great Vancouver]{.text-primary}</h2>

Interact with widgets to find the Dissemination Area that best suits
your needs. Data sourced from multiple public sources, Landsat
satellites, and government websites. Data overview as follows:

<iframe 
  src="https://zijin-xiao.shinyapps.io/DA_Suitability_Explorer_v2/"
  width="100%" 
  height="700px"
  style="border:none;">
</iframe>

For more info, click and download: <br>
https://github.com/Zijin1026/FCOR599-Term1Presentation/blob/main/FinalMap.ipynb


<br>
<br>


<h2>[Image design for reference:]{.text-primary}</h2>

All images are intended for academic purposes, thus prioritizing static
maps. Aesthetically, they favor simplicity, high recognizability, and
dense information content.




![](images/Layout_page-0001.jpg){width="100%"} <br>
<br>
![](images/GEM510_Lab4_page-0001.jpg){width="100%"} <br>
<br>
![](images/LeastCostPath_page-0001.jpg){width="100%"}<br>
<br>
![](images/R2.jpg){width="100%"} <br> 
<br> 
![](images/C&SE.jpg){width="100%"}<br>
<br> 
![](images/Layout.jpg){width="100%"}
